title: Implement BLE transport for Python TUI native client
description: Why this exists: native apps need a direct BLE path for laptop and mobile-like usage where IP over BLE is impractical. What needs to be done: implement BLE scanning, device filtering, connection, service discovery, MTU-aware writes, notification subscription, reconnect handling, timeout handling, and pairing/user-selection behavior using a cross-platform Python BLE library such as Bleak where feasible. Acceptance criteria: works on Linux BlueZ and macOS CoreBluetooth for standard GATT client operations; service/characteristic UUIDs are configurable or derived from the native interface spec; disconnect/reconnect is handled without wedging the UI; tests use mocks/fakes where hardware is absent; hardware smoke-test Bead is created if real-device validation is missing.
notes: Implemented no-hardware Python BLE LCI packet transport. Added BlePacketTransport under python/src/lichen/client/ble.py implementing PacketTransport for SLIP-framed IPv6 over BLE GATT; native LICHEN LCI UUIDs are defaults, legacy NUS is explicit opt-in; lazy optional Bleak dependency exposed via new python extra 'ble'; discovery uses Bleak return_adv advertisement data when available and falls back to metadata; send path chunks writes by characteristic max_write_without_response_size with ATT MTU fallback; notifications feed StreamDecoder; close/reconnect/remote disconnect wake packet consumers and reset per-session queues; BLE I/O/discovery errors are wrapped in BleTransportError. Added fake-client/fake-scanner unit tests covering connect, notify subscription, MTU/max-write chunking, fragmented notification decode, close, remote disconnect, close/reconnect, pre-connect send error, connect failure, notify failure cleanup, write failure wrapping, native discovery default, explicit legacy NUS discovery, advertisement-data discovery, and discovery failure wrapping. Validation on local Python env: cd python && uv run ruff check src/lichen/client tests/client pyproject.toml -> PASS; cd python && uv run mypy src/lichen/client tests/client -> PASS (Success: no issues found in 8 source files); cd python && uv run pytest tests/client -q -> PASS (26 passed in 0.06s); git diff --check -> PASS. EC2/Zephyr not used for this Python-only no-hardware slice. Reviews: two independent reviewers found lifecycle/discovery P2 issues; fixed notify failure cleanup, per-session queue reset, remote disconnect handling, error wrapping, characteristic max-write sizing, and Bleak return_adv discovery. Final reviews from Sartre and Socrates reported no P0/P1/P2 findings. Created child project-LICHEN-py4c.1.5.1 for real Linux BlueZ/macOS CoreBluetooth and physical device smoke tests; blocked because physical BLE hardware/firmware and host adapters are unavailable in this session.
close_reason: Completed software-only BLE LCI packet transport with fake-client/fake-scanner tests and clean reviews; physical BLE validation remains blocked/tracked in project-LICHEN-py4c.1.5.1.
